# ============================================================================

# Pull Request Template

# ============================================================================

## Summary

<!-- Describe the change in 1-3 sentences. -->

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Refactor (no functional change)
- [ ] CI/Build/Infrastructure change
- [ ] Dependency update

## Linked Issues

<!-- Link the issue this PR resolves with `Closes #N` or `Fixes #N`. -->

- Resolves #

## Testing

- [ ] Unit tests pass (`npm test` if applicable)
- [ ] Lint passes (`npm run lint`)
- [ ] Typecheck passes (`npm run typecheck`)
- [ ] Build passes (`npm run build`)
- [ ] `node --version` is **v22.x.x** (run `nvm use` in the repo root; CI runs Node 22 LTS)
- [ ] Manual testing performed (describe below)

### Manual test steps

1.
2.
3.

## Screenshots / Recordings

<!-- For UI changes, attach before/after screenshots. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the project's code style (`lib/`, `app/`, `components/`)
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [ ] I have commented hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have updated documentation if needed
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally
- [ ] Any dependent changes have been merged and published

## Security Considerations

<!-- If this PR touches auth, scanning, payments, or user data, describe any
     security implications. For dependency updates, link the changelog. -->

## Additional Notes

<!-- Anything else reviewers should know: rollout plan, feature flags,
     migration steps, follow-up work, known limitations. -->
